idea-plugin.name: Wiremark
idea-plugin.description:
Sketch user interfaces as plain text and see them rendered as hand-drawn, Balsamiq-style wireframes right inside your IDE. Wiremark brings wiremark — a concise, markdown-embeddable wireframing format (think "YAML-flavored Material UI") — to IntelliJ IDEA, WebStorm, PyCharm, and every other JetBrains IDE.

It renders wiremark in two places:

  • Standalone *.wiremark files open in a split text-and-preview editor with a live, hand-drawn SVG that updates as you type — the same editing experience the IDE gives Markdown and SVG files.
  • Fenced code blocks in Markdown. A ```wireframe (or ```wiremark) block in any .md file renders as a wireframe in the Markdown preview, exactly the way Mermaid diagrams do.

Wireframes are written by indentation, so they read like an outline and diff cleanly in version control. Multi-frame files automatically render as a flow chart, with connectors drawn between screens you link with to=#id. Rendering happens entirely inside the IDE — no Node.js, no external tools, and no network calls.

New to the format? The wiremark documentation has the language guide, component reference, and icon gallery. The engine is open source at github.com/Blackburn-Labs/wiremark.
